segment-text: Written by
(c) 2015 Working Title Films.
Final Shooting Draft
EXT. LONDON SKYLINE - DUSK
PANNING DOWN from the heavens to the city below. Seen from the East End looking west. Traffic in the streets, the Thames, St. Paul's. The BELLS at Bow Church can be heard.
London in the 1960's. Everyone had a story about the Krays. You could walk into any pub to hear a lie or two about them.
EXT. LINCOLN CONTINENTAL - ROLLING THE WEST END - NIGHT
We are low on the rear fender looking up, awash in neon: Ronnie Scott's, the Marquee Club. We catch glimpses of the pretty people passing by.
But I was there and I am not careless with the truth.
INT. LINCOLN CONTINENTAL - ROLLING THE WEST END - NIGHT
In the backseat, the Krays: REGGIE tough and fit, RON off kilter in style. An odd slowness here, like time has stood still. Are they on their way to gangster heaven?
They were brothers. But bound by more than blood, they were twins as well. Counterparts. Gangster princes of the city they meant to conquer.
Ron, heavier, thicker, bespectacled, looks to his brother.
Ron Kray was a one man London mob. Bloodthirsty, illogical, but funny as well.
Reggie looks out the window watching London pass by.
Reggie was different. Once in a lifetime do you find a street fighting man like Reg. Believe me when I say it took a lot of love for me to hate him the way I do.
INT./EXT. 178 VALLANCE ROAD - BETHNAL GREEN - DAY

ON REGGIE KRAY'S back. Follow him as he strides, a boxer's roll to his shoulders. Out onto VALLANCE ROAD. His patch. Full of mischief, Reggie carries TWO TEA CUPS down and across the street to where a sedan is parked. The driver is Constable SCOTT. His passenger is Scotland Yard Inspector NIPPER READ.
Hello, boys. Fancy a cup of tea?
They just look away. Reggie checks out the car.
What's this, the old Dagenham Anglia? Nought to sixty in 20 minutes. It suits you, all black and cramped. (re: tea; to Scott) Milk and sugar? Yes? No?
Shove off, Kray.
Reggie leans low so he can see Nipper.
Hello, Nipper. Don't want to keep you. Not with all the murderers to catch and rapists to lock away.
Nipper just stares. Reggie heads off, dumping the tea in the street as he goes. He hands the cups to Donoghue who waits over by a sleek looking, powerful FORD GALAXY 500.
Where's my driver? Where's Frank?
He ain't here. Can't get out of bed in the morning, Reg. His heart ain't in it.
Well, let's go get him then. Ormsby Street, right?
Donoghue nods, opens the passenger door. Reg shakes his head. Final Shooting Draft
No, I could use the walk. Lovely day for it.
Reggie turns, smiles at and motions Nipper to follow.
Come on then!
As Reggie starts down Vallance Road, Donoghue sighs, shakes his head and gets inside the Galaxy.
INT. ANGLIA - ROLLING DOWN VALLANCE ROAD - DAY
Nipper watching from the Anglia as the Galaxy (driven by Donoghue) follows Reggie who strolls along. He tussles a KID'S hair, greets an OLD WOMAN. He's GREETED back warmly.
Eastenders. They won't talk to a policeman, but they'll kiss a gangster.
As Nipper shakes his head in disgust...
EXT. HOUSE - ORMSBY STREET - BETHNAL GREEN - DAY
Reggie knocks on the door. And FRANCES SHEA answers, the future Mrs. Kray. Reggie looks at her a beat.
Is Frank about?
She considers and after a moment calls back over her shoulder into the house. Toward the hallway stairs behind her. Frank!
Reggie really checks her out now.
Who are you?
Frances. Frank's sister.
Final Shooting Draft
Hang on, I know you. But you were just a girl. You've all grown up, haven't you? Frances is rolling something around in her mouth. It happens.
What have you got there?
Meaning her mouth. She laughs, takes a CANDY out of her mouth with her thumb and forefinger, shows it to Reggie. A sweet.
Lemon sherbet? (Frances nods) Gives us a few sucks on it?
He opens his mouth. She smiles, drops it in. Reggie rolls it around in his mouth, nods at the taste.
Oh, that's nice. But a bit early, isn't it?
Mum still makes me take a tonic in the morning. Sweet's the only thing that'll chase off the taste.
Reggie crunches. Frances reacts.
You're not supposed to chew it.
No? How else am I supposed to get at the sherbetty bit?
By being patient.
No, patience doesn't get you what you want.
Final Shooting Draft
The sound of a window raising. Reggie looks up to see FRANK SHEA looking down from the second floor. In a T-shirt and bedheaded. Horrified when he sees who is waiting.
Half a minute, Reg. Right down.
Frank ducks back in. Reggie frowns, checks his watch.
Is my brother in trouble?
(nods; then...) Would you go out with me? I'll take it easy on him if you do.
Yes, but not for that reason.
Saturday night?
She nods again. Suddenly, her mother MRS. SHEA is there.
Who are you talking to?
She sees Reggie, obviously doesn't think much of him.
You're half dressed talking to a man in the door. What's wrong with you, Frances? Get back in the kitchen. Finish them dishes.

Frances shrugs at Reggie who takes the sweet from his mouth, offers it back. Her mother watches as she takes it, pops it in her mouth as she goes. Reggie smiles rakishly. Mrs. Shea slams the door in his face.
INT. FORD GALAXY 500 - DAY (ROLLING)
Frank drives. Donoghue beside him. Reggie coiled in the back. Silent. Frank obviously nervous, concentrating on the road.
Oversleep again, Frank, and it's back to the cheese shop or the chip shop or whatever shop you came from.
Final Shooting Draft
Print. Print shop.
Do you understand what I'm saying? You'll be headed to the shit shop. (after a beat) What does your sister do?
She goes to one of those Pitman Colleges. Typing, shorthand.
Donoghue shaking his head, looks back for the Anglia.
Girl's got a bit of class, mate. How come I haven't seen her? Where you been hiding her?
She's been away. (a beat) She's a bit fragile, Reg. Yeah?
Frank just nods and that's the end of it. As they drive...
Stop here, Frank.
As Frank pulls to the curb...
EXT. NARROW LANE - DAY (SAME)
As Donoghue and Reggie exit the Galaxy, start walking. The Anglia pulls in fifty feet behind.
(shouts to Nipper) Five minutes! Won't be long!
From Nipper's angle it looks like they're entering a BUILDING. But from Reggie and Donoghue's angle... They've entered a...
NARROW WALKING ALLEY
They're giving Nipper the slip. Frowning. Realizing. Suddenly bolting out of the tiny Anglia. MOVE WITH NIPPER as he hurries down the pavement, ducks down the walking alley. Emerges onto...
A SECOND ALLEY
Reggie and Donoghue nowhere in sight. As Nipper realizes he's been had...
Looks nearly at camera. As Ron speaks we cannot see to whom.

And I'm staring at the back of this geezer's head. Drilling him with my eyes. The whole time thinking, give Ron Kray your sausage. Give Ron your fucking sausage. Until I'm not thinking it anymore; I'm broadcasting it, Ron Kray sausage, through his skull and into his brain. Till he looks back at me and says, "Ron, do you fancy my sausage? I got no appetite this morning." (lets it sink in) Interesting, ain't it?
When my future brother-in-law said interesting, it meant he had no fucking idea what he, or you, or anyone else was talking about.
WIDEN: Ron sits across from Reggie and Donoghue in the...
INT. DAY ROOM AT LONG GROVE HOSPITAL - DAY
Ron has glasses, wears a comfy old jumper. All the patients here in their own clothes. Reggie looks at Donoghue: his brother is mad as a March hare.
Ron had been sentenced to three years in prison for Grievous Bodily Harm. (MORE)
Near the end of his sentence he was certified insane and remanded to a mental hospital. What was wanting was a friendly psychiatrist.
BIG PAT CONNOLLY LYING ON A PSYCHIATRIST'S COUCH Big Pat is, in fact, enormous Pat. DR. HUMPHRIES frowns as the couch creaks under the big man's weight.
When you say you want to raspberry this man, I'm not sure of your vernacular. What do you mean? BIG PAT Raspberry ripple - cripple.
You want to cripple someone?
Yep, that's right.
And why is that?
Because, doctor, I'm afraid he may not be inclined to provide a favorable evaluation of my employer.
Said softly, his sausage size fingers laced over his chest.
And who is your employer?
Ronnie Kray. Is there hope, Doctor?
Excuse me a moment.
RECEPTION AREA
Humphries enters, starts purposely toward his SECRETARY. He stops short at the sight of a second ENORMOUS MAN sitting in his waiting area. He's cracking his huge knuckles.
Who is that?
Final Shooting Draft
Another new patient. (whispers) Tommy 'the Bear of Tottenham' Brown.
As Humphries considers his options...
INT. MEETING ROOM - LONG GROVE HOSPITAL - DAY
Humphries stands to address the PSYCHIATRIC BOARD. Seated at a table behind him is Ron who stares.
Ronald Kray possesses possibly the soundest mind I've seen in twentysix years of psychiatric practice. In my view to keep him here would be a waste of public resources.
Humphries slumps back down in his chair, mutters to himself:
A lifetime's integrity down the toilet.
Ron looks over at him, sure that he willed it...
The Queen would survive, but God save the rest of us.
INT. LONG GROVE HOSPITAL - DAY
Reggie and Donoghue walking toward the exit. Mr. Kray! VOICE They both turn as DOCTOR HUMPHRIES heads briskly over, fresh from the hearing.
I hope you know what you're doing.
I know exactly what I'm doing. Driving back to London.
Final Shooting Draft
Your brother has no real idea who or what he is. Nor does he trust his own senses. I've never seen a man so desperately in need of reassurance.
Well, we all like a compliment now and then, don't we?
This is not a joke. Ron exists in a fantasy world. He's a man in a house of mirrors.
Do you understand what he's on about, Albert?
Donoghue shakes his head, neither giving the Doc an inch.
What I'm on about is your brother is arbitrary, violent and psychopathic. Probably paranoid schizophrenic. What I'm trying to tell you is he's off his spinning top!
Humphries steps up, takes Reggie's hand and slaps a BOTTLE OF PILLS down, angrily explains:
It's called Stematol. You need to give it to your brother twice a day. Twice a day or there's going to be fucking trouble.
MIRROR (FRANCES' BEDROOM)
CLOSE ON Frances' as she applies a FALSE EYELASH. Bright and young and beautiful. As she flutters her eyes, frowns...
Frances! The car is here!
INT. HALLWAY - SHEA HOUSE - EVENING
Frances coming down the stairs wearing a stylish pants suit. Her brother Frank looks her over as she turns a pirouette. Final Shooting Draft
You've got glamour, Frances. You're like an East End starlet.
Mrs. Shea arrives, wearing an apron and drying her hands. MRS. SHEA
As Frances reacts to the stinging words...
They're trousers, Mum. She doesn't look like a harlot.
What she's wearing doesn't matter.
When people see her with Reggie Kray, they'll think she's a tart.
Why would you say that?!
He's a gangster, Frances! The Krays are gangsters!
As Mrs. Shea stomps away, Frances watches after her...
I think he's sweet! And I'm going to kiss him, do you hear me?!
REGGIE - OUTSIDE THE SHEA HOUSE - EVENING (SAME)

Leaning against his CONTINENTAL, a bit of danger in a sharp suit. Reggie heard Frances' declaration, absorbs it and... Frances exits the front door. She gives nothing away. As they consider each other... Hello.
You look lovely, Frances. So do you.
EXT. THE DOUBLE R CLUB - NIGHT

A drab East End street. The Continental pulls up and Reggie gets out, opens the door for Frances. MOVE WITH them as they cross to where a 300-pound Glasgwegian doorman stands. It's Big Pat from the psychiatrist's office.
Hello, Big Pat. BIG PAT
Big Pat's here to keep the riff raff out. (Pat nods sagely) So how come you're letting us in?
Frances laughs. We FOLLOW THEM past Big Pat and inside into a whole other world...
INT. DOUBLE R CLUB - OUTER ROOM - NIGHT
An eclectic crowd. Reggie greets Firm member IAN BARRIE who's parked at the bar. As Reggie continues to lead Frances further in, anyone who sees him says 'Hello'.
Everyone here knows you.
Of course. This is my club.
You mean it's your local?
I mean I own the place.
I've been by here ten times; I never knew it was yours.
You can get away with an awful lot in life if you don't shout about it.
They enter...
THE INNER ROOM

The place is hopping. A SINGER on a small stage croons The Look Of Love. Reggie pauses to greet a couple of flat-faced BOXERS. As he continues with Frances, she leans in, whispers: FRANCES Those men look frightening.
No. They're just old boxers. I let them drink for free if they behave.
Proper East End mugs. A BEAUTIFUL WOMAN steps past, smiles at Reggie as she passes.
Reggie, that was Joan Collins.
Yeah, and Barbara Windsor was in here last night. Really?
That's my formula. Mix celebrities with a villain or two. The punters love it. It's really good for business.
They stop at a table with a great view of the stage.
Would you like a seat?
Frances smiles as he pulls out her chair. But as she sits -RONNIE HART
No, I haven't.
The barman passes Reggie the drinks. Wincing, Hart presses.
It's important.
Something about the way Hart says it. Reggie can't ignore him. He smiles at Frances. Final Shooting Draft
Frances, would you mind if I take care of some business for a moment?
No, of course not.
Reggie follows as Ronnie Hart leads the way. MOVE WITH him and an annoyed Reggie around the corner, down a hall into...
THE BACK BAR
Where a nervous, shambling JACK 'the Hat' MCVITIE sits in a booth with Donoghue. He grimaces a smile when he sees Reggie.
How are you, Reg?
No complaints, Jack. But who'd listen anyway? What's the palaver?
Reggie sits. Jack wedged between him and Donoghue with Hart
You had a question about the take on the Purple Heart business. The pill game. Jack here would like to explain.
McVitie gulps, trying to appear cool.
Well, Reg, I, um, the thing is, under normal circumstances, I -REGGIE For Chrissakes get on with it. I've got things to do.
I borrowed a few quid off the top. Sold a few pills on the side. Things have been tight at home; and I got carried away trying to keep the wife happy.
Exasperated, Reggie looks from Hart to Donoghue.
You brought me in here for this?
Final Shooting Draft
You should know about this.
Reggie disgusted with McVitie, looks him over.
You be at work tomorrow. You pay back every penny. Understood?
Yes. Of course.
McVitie hoping he's in the clear as Reggie takes out a pack of cigarettes, expertly taps one out which he takes with his mouth. He looks to McVitie, sighs. Cigarette?

McVitie nods. Reggie taps out another, holds it face level. As McVitie leans forward, opens his mouth to take it -WHALLOP! Reggie arcs a right hook into his jaw, breaking it. McVitie down in a heap. Reggie looms over him.
I've marked your card, Jack. Next time I'll finish you off.
McVitie moans, manages a nod.
Now if you'll excuse me, I've got better things to do tonight.
Reggie goes. Donoghue catches up with him.
There's something else.
Reggie stops short, cuts him off.
I'm on a night out, Albert. I've got a young lady with me and I'm entertaining. (points through door) There. Do you see that?
I see.
It can wait until tomorrow.

Final Shooting Draft Donoghue gives up. WE FOLLOW Reggie back out as he brushes off his suit, leaving the ugly encounter behind, returning to his charming self as... Reggie joins her, like nothing just happened.
Did I miss anything? Just me.
That's a lot to miss. Is it?
Yeah. Your brother was telling me you're studying to be a secretary.
Anything to get out of the East End.
What's wrong with the East End?
I've got bigger dreams than pushing a pram or haggling with the butcher. I just don't know what they are yet.
The East End's not so bad. The center of the world can be anywhere you like.
Even here? In Bethnal Green?
Reggie nods, looks her over, likes what he sees.
Do you like being a gangster? That stops him short. He gathers, answers...
I'm a club owner.
Final Shooting Draft She waits for more. And something about her, brings out a bit of truth.
I like the money and the respect, of being a club owner. She smiles at the euphemism.

I could've been a boxer, but I might've ended up punchy, not knowing who I was, instead of knowing it for sure. (a beat) Mostly I like the fact that I've made a little something of myself. I don't owe the world a thing.
So long as the world agrees with you.
He looks at her a long beat. And finally Frances leans in and KISSES him.
EXT. WOODS - DAY
Moving through the trees, Winston Churchill's voice in the air. A strange incongruity.
We shall fight on the beaches. We shall fight on the landing grounds.
A CARAVAN ahead, nestled in a hollow. As we MOVE IN on it Churchill's voice is coming from inside.
We shall fight in the fields and in the streets. We shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender.
REVEAL Reggie approaching, leading Frances by the hand.
Ron, come out! You've got visitors!
A needle scratch as the Churchill recording stops.
Final Shooting Draft
The door opens. There's Ron, slightly disheveled, but trying not to look it. He smiles up as Reggie leads her over.
Hullo, Frances. Welcome to the family.
He looks to Reggie who's a bit embarrassed at the thought.
I mean, maybe one day.
Reggie gestures toward the caravan.
Have you got a bottle of beer in there? (Ron nods) I'll let you two get to know each other.
As Reggie heads inside, Ron looks to Frances.
You want a cup of tea?
He leads her to where a kettle sits on a grate over a fire.
(re: Reggie) So, do you think we look alike? Certainly.
No, you think Reggie is beautiful and I'm not.
As Frances tries to figure out how to answer...
(from inside) Unload the car! Be useful!
A lithe young man bounds out, smiles fraternally at Frances as he heads off for the Lincoln. This is 'MAD' TEDDY SMITH. Ron watches after him, turns back to Frances.
I'm a homosexual, Frances.
As she reacts, he clarifies. Final Shooting Draft
I'm a giver not a receiver mind you. I believe you shouldn't to hide what you are, it makes you sad; it makes you unwell.
I agree completely.
He considers her, confides...
(low; conspiratorial) Me and my brother are going to rule London.
As Frances reacts to this oath, Ron switches gears.
Do you eat raw eggs?
No, no, I don't.
You should. Raw eggs make you good at sex.
As Frances tries to find her footing...
INT. OFFICE - SOUTH LONDON SCRAP YARD - DAY

MCLEAN hangs upside down from the ceiling, two electrodes clipped to his nipples. EDDIE RICHARDSON looms, the prosecutor. CHARLIE RICHARDSON sit at a table, the judge. ALBERT WOODS and other GANG MEMBERS are the jury. GEORGE CORNELL stands by a hand-crank-powered GENERATOR.
The Krays' main rivals were the Richardsons of South London. They were called the torture gang.
(to McLean) For the benefit of the jury, on whose behalf were you operating on this side of the river?
With Eddie circling around him, McLean appeals to Charlie.
Please, Charlie, I'm begging you.
No. You do business with the court as a entity. Therefore address the court. What?
MCLEAN
Eddie belts him. McLean refocuses from the blow.
Your honor. By saying your honor you gain the court's attention and open a channel of communication.
So again, on whose behalf were you working on this side of the river?
Please, Charlie, I'm fucking begging. I don't know what you're on about.
Charlie bangs a PIPE WRENCH GAVEL.
Order in this my court!
WHACK! Eddie belts McLean again. Charlie steps over, gives McLean a push. As McLean slowly swings...

You're in Indian Country, old son. That's what you Cockneys call south of the river, isn't it? Indian country? What are you doing on my reservation, without my say so, filling up your canteen from my fucking watering hole?
You haven't got the bottle to do that on your own. Who do you work for?
I don't know what you're on about -Charlie nods to Cornell --
Buzz him.
Objection, your honor, my client is an upstanding citizen.
Objection overruled on the grounds it's boring.
Cornell shrugs, cranks the generator. MCLEAN goes rigid as electricity courses through him.
Say it! Say the Krays, you daft cunt!
No leading the witness, Eddie!
Cornell eases off the juice. McLean sags...
You're scared of the wrong outfit. And now you're on the wrong side of the river and the wrong side of me. You've fallen foul of the Cherokee. And you know what Indians do to cowboys, don't you?
Charlie whispers a threat into McLean's ear. Whatever it is, it works...
The Krays! I work for the Krays!
EXT. SWEETS SHOP - DAY
Reggie scanning through the window, spotting a jar of LEMON SHERBETS. As he smiles...
EXT. SWEETS SHOP - DAY

Reggie exits. He starts crossing the street carrying a small WHITE PAPER BAG of sweets. A CAR zooms forward, barrels at him with intent. Cornell and Albert Woods seen through the windscreen. Final Shooting Draft Reggie wheels, leaps up, landing on the hood before slamming to the pavement. As the car careen by, Reggie has managed not to let go of the sweets.
INT. GREEK RESTAURANT - SOUTH LONDON - DAY
Where Eddie & Charlie Richardson sit by themselves, scarfing down enormous gyros.
Alexander may have been great, but he wasn't a Greek. No?
No, he was a Macedonian, but he loved 'em, Aristotle and that lot. He was deeply impressed with those flash Hellenistic cunts.
Now all that's left are gyros, hummus and stuffed grape leaves.
And democracy, Eddie, don't forget democracy.

Suddenly the wall explodes as a BOX VAN crashes through. Eddie and Charlie are lost in a storm of debris. Finally, all we hear is an ENGINE RACING. Eddie emerges, looks in the empty van to see the gas pedal is wedged down. Charlie shake off some dust, lets loose a HOWL of rage. As a section of the ceiling drops down behind him...
The Richardson's sent George Cornell to call for a truce.
INT. PELLICCI'S CAFE - DAY
Reggie and Ron at a table having breakfast. Cornell and cohort MIKE JOBBER enter. They're intercepted by Donoghue, Barrie and Big Pat.
Whoa, that's far enough, mate.
Final Shooting Draft
We're here to parlay.
Barrie opens a flick knife.
I will map you up, Southie.
No. Let 'em through.
They do so, following them closely. Cornell to Reggie:
Things've gone far enough. Charlie wants a meet. The Pig & Whistle. Neutral ground, just him, Eddie and you two.
And how do we know we'll be safe?
Safe? Well, that's a little thing called trust, ain't it?
When one fella starts to talk about trust, look over your shoulder and you'll see another getting ready to shove his cock right up your ass.
Well, you wouldn't mind that, would you, you fat poof?
Ron draws a GUN. Reggie grabs his arm.
No, Ron, not here. (to Cornell) An hour it is. Now shove off!
Cornell offers one last leer at Ron before he and Jobber start back. Big Pat and Barrie see them off.
I don't like it, Reggie.
I know that pub. And them. We'll be alright.
INT. PIG & WHISTLE - DAY
A DOZEN CUSTOMERS. Reggie and Ron enter, looking about. They head to the bar. Reggie holds up two fingers to the BARMAN.
Two Guinness.
Half a minute. Gotta go down and change the barrel.

Obviously rattled, he disappears down the hole in the floor to the cellar. As he pulls the trap door down over his head --- Four customers scurry out. The other EIGHT are RICHARDSON GANGSTERS. Approaching from all sides. Tough bastards. Tooled up with coshes and pipes. One, Mike Jobber, holds a razor.
Reg, Ron, the Richardsons were unexpectedly engaged. We're going to look after you.
(re: Jobber's razor) What's that supposed to be?
What do you think? It's a tool.
No it's not; it's a fucking utensil. What are you going to do with that, fry me an egg?
Ron indicates some hard metal shoved down in his pockets.
I came here for a shoot out. A proper shoot out with some proper men! Like Colonel Custer and
(turns) Reg, this lot are fucking nonces. (to Jobber) Get out of my way.
Motioning, with what seem to be guns, Ron walks past the Richardson gang and pauses by the front door.
Final Shooting Draft
A shoot-out is a fucking shoot out! Like a Western...
And then he's gone. One of the gang closes the door behind him. Jobber and the others turn back to Reggie.
You're brother's done a runner.
(shrugs) You don't mind if I pour myself a pint, do you?
Reggie reaches over the bar, starts to POUR a GUINNESS. Jobber trades incredulous looks with the others.
Charlie Richardson said we're to knock the granny out of you, Reg.
All the while Reggie tends to his Guinness.

He did? Well... (turns) Fuck Charlie Richardson. And his brother. Fuck Georgie Cornell. And Albert Woods. Fuck you, hmm? And fuck the whole fucking lot of you. (holds up glass) First pour about three quarters. Then you let it settle.
The eight close in a step. Reggie sets the glass on the bar.
Before you top it off proper. You want the head looking proud over the glass.
Reggie shoves his hands in his pockets, rocks on his toes like a proud schoolboy.
It won't bother you cunts if I fight back, will it?
If you think you can manage it.
It won't exactly be by the rules.
Final Shooting Draft

Reggie pulls his hands out of his pockets, both fists held tight around BRASS KNUCKLES. LAUGHS all around. They're not intimidated. Only Reggie sees: RON quietly slip back into the bar from the OTHER ENTRANCE, behind them, a HAMMER in each hand. Reggie smiles.
You think I'm joking? Try this one
then. A paranoid schizophrenic walks
into a bar...

Ron doesn't break stride as SWING! Blind-sides the rear man. Jobber crumples even as Reggie wades in. And it's one-two, from Reggie, the brass knuckles covered in blood as he keeps an unconscious men standing with a flurry of uppercuts.
When the Krays fought together, they could defeat an army. Style-wise it was chalk and cheese, but you couldn't argue with the results.

We watch the Kray Brothers obliterate eight men. They fight forward, meeting in the middle until the last of the Richardson gang is finished off. As Ron adds finishing touches to those writhing on the floor... A satisfied Reggie steps to his Guinness, takes a sip.
They never stood a chance against my beautiful Reggie.
Reggie winces at Ron's savagery...
INT. SOUTH LONDON SCRAP YARD - DAY
Charlie alone watching a small TV.
Gang war seemed certain, but on the morning of the World Cup Final, Scotland Yard had its say as well.
Charlie leaps to his feet as Geoff Hurst scores.
And as POLICE suddenly pour in to arrest him....
EXT./INT. LINCOLN CONTINENTAL - NIGHT
Parked. The windows fogged up. Reggie and Frances kissing like they just invented it.
Charlie Richardson was arrested and sentenced to 25 years for fraud, extortion and grievous bodily harm.
Finally, Frances pulls back. She considers him as she catches her breath. He considers her in return.
EXT. KENSINGTON GORE - DAY
STEFAN DE FAYE walks cheerily along. Young and bright, he checks an address on a business card, enters a BUILDING.
London was an open city and the
Krays moved in.
A POV IN A HALLWAY
On a SECRETARY'S BACK approaching double wooden doors. As she swings them open, Stefan De Faye passes and we are now in... THE OFFICE LESLIE PAYNE smiles from his desk, looking good in a suit.
Mr. de Faye, glad you could come.
(steps forward) Mr. Payne. I'm not often intrigued, but your message, it intrigued.
Please. Sit down.
Final Shooting Draft
As de Faye sits, he doesn't notice the two men sitting against the wall behind him: Ron & Reggie Kray.
(gestures) These are the Kray twins...
As de Faye turns, clocks them...
...Ronald and Reginald.
(nervous) Yes, I've, heard of them, of you.
You'll find them useful friends.
The vibe shifts on its axis as Ron blows a smoke ring, doesn't look over. Reggie offers a nod, goes back to checking his fingernails. De Faye frightened now.
What's this about, Mr. Payne?
This is about a gambling casino in
Knightsbridge, Esmeralda's Barn.
I don't think I'm familiar with it.

Then you'll be shocked to learn you earned 24,000 pounds from it in the last three months. That you're one of four principal investors. But that final control is vested in a company called Hotel Organization Ltd. Did you know that company is you, and you alone, Mr. De Faye?
De Faye looks back at the Krays, to Payne, then finally nods.
My friends and I wish to purchase the casino from you. I have the contracts ready for your signature.
De Faye looks at a DOCUMENT facing him, a PEN alongside. Final Shooting Draft
Ruling London involved intelligence and intimidation.

Elections are in ten weeks. They'll vote out both sides of the House. (a beat) What are you doing about it?
EXT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - GERRARD STREET - NIGHT Christmas. The West End glitters, bustling with life. Frances
walks arm-in-arm with Reggie. They stop across from the club.
What do you want for Christmas?
You don't have to get me anything.
What? How about a lump of coal?
As Frances laughs, Reggie takes her to the side, points at the CLUB.

Reggie, it's snowing...
She catches a FLAKE in her palm, watches it melt, smiles. Gone...
FRANCES (CONT'D)
And as they head off, a TIME LAPSE takes us to a deserted DAY TIME view of the place.
What did I want for Christmas? My Reggie free and above board. But Ron wanted his Reggie back as well. (MORE)
He longed for his gangster twin. And Reggie? He chose a hideaway instead.
INT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - GERRARD STREET - DAY
HEW MCCOWAN sits across from Reggie and Payne much as de Faye did earlier. McCowan looks frightened,; he's obviously had things explained to him.

The owner was Hew McCowan the son of a baronet. Though he was not usually intimidated by the working classes, he agreed to sell half the club. Reggie knew he'd get the rest in time. A dirty deal for a clean life. As McCowan signs the contract that Payne offers...
INT. CARPENTERS ARMS PUB - DAY
Ron behind the bar in a fury, throwing PINT GLASSES. Teddy Smith and Leslie Holt duck for cover.
Ron was terrified of Reggie leaving him behind. He sent Mad Teddy Smith round to kill the deal.
INT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - SOHO - NIGHT
McCowan protesting to Teddy Smith and Leslie Holt. Teddy sips from a champagne glass.
Listen, gentlemen, I have a deal with Reggie
This tastes shit.
Teddy Smith tosses the glass to the floor.
I've signed the contract.
Now a bottle hits the floor. Final Shooting Draft
Please, I have an arrangement with Reggie. Ask him.
Leslie Holt follows as Teddy marches along the bar sweeping every bottle and glass shattering to the floor. Startled PATRONS step back and away.
We don't work for Reggie! We work for Ronnie!
Behind the bar Hew McCowan dials a number on the phone.

INT. VISITING WARD - BRIXTON GAOL - DAY
Frances across from Reggie. She's been crying. He tries to catch her eye, but she won't look at him.
Frankie, the police stitched me up. I'm innocent.
Even if that's true, you're back in prison. You made a promise to me.
I'm fucking innocent!
He rages it at her. Frances looks at him, blinks.
I'm sorry, I didn't -(gathers himself) I want you to marry me, Frankie...
If you're innocent and acquitted, I'll marry you. If you're guilty, I never want to see you again
As he blinks at her back at her...
It was Lord Boothby who saved the day. His walk on the wild side had not gone unnoticed.
PAYNE & REGGIE - BRIXTON GAOL VISITING WARD
Reggie breathes a sigh as Payne tells him...
I found the photo. It's been
forwarded to the proper places.
INT. BOOTHBY'S FLAT - BELGRAVIA - DAY
Ron, Boothby and Leslie Holt sat on the couch. Teddy Smith stepping up with a camera. Say Enugu.
BOOTHBY
The frame freezing into a BLACK & WHITE PHOTO as Teddy takes the shot.
A photograph taken in Boothby's flat
EXT. NEWSTAND - PICCADILLY CIRCUS - DAY
THE SUNDAY MIRROR headline: The Picture We Dare Not Print. As one after another is plucked off the stack...
-- Along with a story of sexual misconduct between a gangster and a member of the House of Lords...
HAROLD WILSON - AT HIS DESK
Looks from the headline to the formidable ARNOLD GOODMAN. Final Shooting Draft
Unfortunately, Arnold, it's all true for once.
Wilson holds out the paper. Goodman waves it off.
I don't care about the truth, Harold, except the greater the truth, the greater the libel. May I use your phone?
Wilson nods. As Goodman dials...
An election was looming so Prime Minister Wilson brought in his fixer
Arnold Goodman to sort things out.
INT. KENSINGTON GORE OFFICE - DAY
Payne flipping through a paper, the Sunday Mirror headline: To Lord Boothby, An Unequivocal Apology.
The great and the good engineered a cover up. A cracking good one...
INT. THE OLD BAILEY - DAY
Ron and Reggie in the dock. Frances in the gallery. Nipper watching from the wings. Hew McCowan stands as a witness. A NOTE is walked in to the JUDGE by a CLERK.

When the Krays' defense threatened to call Boothby as a character witness, the implication was clear. The government would have to drop its case or face the consequences. It was this that made the Krays the untouchables of London crime... The judge absorbs the note, announces:
New information casts poor light on the prosecution witness Mr. McCowan. Case dismissed!
Final Shooting Draft
As the courtroom erupts -- Ron turns beaming to Reggie, but Reggie looks back to where Frances sits. As she smiles... Nipper marches out.
That night the Krays held their celebration party at McCowan's club. Reggie had bought the Hideaway that same afternoon. At a discount.
(singing) Now, I'm sorry if I hurt you...
INT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - GERRARD STREET - NIGHT

Dozens of champagne corks pop! A glittering, packed house as the boys celebrate. Here with Donoghue, the Firm, their friends, Les Payne and MRS. PAYNE. Everyone dressed to kill. Oohs and ahhs as Timi Yuro belts it out:
Make the world go away, get it off my shoulder. Say the things you used to say, and make the world go away.

She serenades Reggie and Frances who sits very close beside him. They look fantastic. Frances trying not to blush at all the attention. Timi singing like there's no tomorrow. Frances mouths "I love you" and they KISS to a CHEER from the crowd. Even Ron concedes the moment with a mollifying shrug.
EXT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - GERRARD STREET - NIGHT
Nipper outside, watches CELEBRANTS enter the raucous club. Fuming, Nipper decides... Heads in.
INT. THE HIDEAWAY CLUB - SOHO - NIGHT
People dance. Nipper looks like a soldier lost behind enemy lines. A WAITRESS offers champagne off a tray she carries. Nipper looks her off as Reggie steps up, rather untroubled.
What are you doing here then?
Final Shooting Draft
I came to see the difference between you and me.
The difference? Don't you know?
I grew up poor, same as you. I boxed as a lad, same as you. But it didn't turn me into a thief.
No, it turned you to policing, to dragging your own before the bar. The difference between us is... I work for me and you, you work for them.

Ron comes up from the side, throws an arm over Nipper's shoulder. As Nipper looks to see who it is -Reggie throws an arm around his other shoulder. They smile as - a CLUB PHOTOGRAPHER FLASH goes off. And as Nipper blinks, he finds himself standing alone...
A PHOTO OF NIPPER, REGGIE & RON AT THE HIDEAWAY
Superintendant Cummings holds it up for Nipper to see. Nipper stands at attention across the desk from him.
I can explain, sir.

Your investigation into the Krays will end at once.
Cummings exits. As Nipper heavily takes his seat...
Final Shooting Draft EXT. ST. JAMES THE LESSER - BETHNAL GREEN - DAY ROLLERS gleam double-parked down Bethnal Green Road. Famous

BOXERS sign autographs before entering. WOMEN in big hats, it's a Cockney Ascot. Reggie having a last drag on a cigarette before heading toward the entrance. He stops short at the sight of Frances' mum Mrs. Shea. She's all in BLACK and looks as grim as a reaper. Donoghue steps up beside Reggie.
She thought the invite said funeral.
Black... Fucking bitch.
As Mrs. Shea spots him, looks through him...
Forget it, Reggie. Her daughter looks fantastic in white. Come on.
INT. ST. JAMES THE LESSER - DAY

The FIRM gathered GIRLFRIENDS & WIVES. Big Pat. Reggie standing at the front of the church with Ron. All eyes on the door as the arrival of the bride is imminent. Reggie looks nervous and uncomfortable. Ron looks him over, then:
(whispers) What do you see in her?
(whispers back) I see myself. I see how I could be... If I wasn't afraid.
But you're not afraid of anything.
Only myself... And you.
EXT. CHURCH ENTRANCE - DAY
Frances here in white, the BRIDAL ROLLER arrived below. Mrs. Shea here along with Frank. Final Shooting Draft
Are you absolutely sure you want to go through with this? I love him.

I know it's not the answer to anything. But love is a witness. Reggie sees me. And I see him. (softly) And who are you to refuse that?

I'm your mother.
INT. ST. JAMES THE LESSER - BETHNAL GREEN - DAY
Reggie looks up. All heads turning as the 'Wedding March' begins. Here comes Frances up the aisle, escorted by Frank.

Oh, Ron, she looks beautiful. FATHER ALBERT FOSTER - ST. JAMES THE LESSER At the altar in mid-prayer. Reggie and Frances before him. FATHER FOSTER ...Through Jesus Christ. Amen. Organ music starts for a hymn: All Love Excelling. Ron SINGS in full throat as do most of the congregation.
Fix in us thy humble Dwelling, All thy faithful Mercies crown...
Ron looks to the bride's side. Tight lipped Mrs. Shea does not sing at all. Ron marches over to her, glares, hisses... Final Shooting Draft
Sing, fuck you, sing. FRANCES Beautiful behind her veil. Oblivious to Ron.
We honeymooned in Greece. The Parthenon had stood for 2,400 years; Reggie's promise to go straight lasted two weeks.
INT. KITCHEN - 176 VALLANCE ROAD - DAY
Donoghue is the last in a line of lieutenants dropping STACKS OF CASH into the kitchen sink as Reggie looks on.
The Two Aces, the New Mill, then the Gigi Club, Ronnie Scott's of course. The sink is three quarters full as Payne opens a briefcase over it, heaping things. There must be 100,000 pounds here.
The bearer bond cashed in Hamburg.
As a HUZZAH goes up...
Kettle's on the boil. It's tea.
INT. BEDROOM - CEDRA COURT - NIGHT
Frances in bed alone staring at the ceiling. Music plays up there. People are dancing, laughing. All the voices male.

We started out near Marble Arch in West London. But we both missed the East End. And so we took an empty flat below Ron's at Cedra Court. Like all else in life it was supposed to be temporary. Not very clever. Ron's parties would keep me awake at night and Clubland kept Reggie out until all hours. He enjoyed being a gangster after all.
INT. CARPENTERS ARMS PUB - DAY
Jack 'the Hat' McVitie stands before Reggie, Big Pat beyond.
How's the Black Bomber business, Jack?
(smiles) Can't complain, Reg. Who'd listen?

Those bastard Barry Brothers blocked me coming in. Said I was drunk.
Reggie takes out a pack of cigarettes.

I spiced the evening up for people.
Reggie LAUGHS, taps out a cigarette, offers it. The trick McVitie fell for before. Smiling, hoping for the best, he leans in, mouths it - WHALLOP! As Jack hits the floor...
You never fucking learn.
As Big Pat looks down, realizes to his chagrin that a drop of Jack's blood has splattered his tie... Bollocks...
INT. KITCHEN - 178 VALLANCE ROAD - DAY
Frances sets down a pot of tea on the kitchen table where Violet and Ron sit waiting.
So kind of you. What a treat.
Final Shooting Draft
It's lovely.
Violet pours a cup, frowns at the stream. She stops, sniffs her cup, then tastes. Ron watches with mock concern. Violet looks at Frances, quite disappointed.
Oh no, Frances, that won't do. Go
sit with Mr. Kray; I'll sort it out. As Frances heads out, Violet stands, starts fussing.
Dear God, she can't even make a decent cup of tea.
Poor Reggie.
LIVING ROOM
As Frances sits across from creepy Charles Kray Sr. who nurses a FLASK. Frances can easily overhear from the kitchen: RON'S VOICE She looks a mess in that dress.
A rag and bone man wouldn't pick her
up if she was lain in the gutter. Charles leers at her as he offers his flask.
EXT. FRONT DOOR - 178 VALLANCE ROAD - DAY
Frances exits, softly closes the door. She starts away.
I left without saying goodbye, a sad victory. I would never be good enough for their Reggie. He was Nipper's Reggie as well.
Crossing the street, she's close to running as she passes the

They watch her go. Read has little sympathy.
Wherever you're going, don't stop till you reach the sea.

We see Frances a beat before she opens it. As she selects a BOTTLE OF PILLS...
But we all need something and what I shared with Ron was needing pills to get through the day. Sleeping pills helped sometimes. Other pills helped other times.
She shakes out a few, goes about taking them.
They were like a friend who held no judgement and only wanted to be loved in return. Mother's little helpers, they were very well named.
A RED TRIUMPH SPITFIRE CONVERTIBLE
Top down, gleaming, brand new, parked at the curb. Frances walks this way from Cedra Court, her eyes closed, led by Reggie who holds her hand. They stop in front of the car.
Alright. Open up.
She opens her eyes, takes in the vehicle.
Happy Birthday, Frankie.
(very pleased) What kind? What's the name of it?
Triumph. Triumph Spitfire.
Spitfire... Can I sit in it?
Final Shooting Draft
It's yours, Frankie. You can do whatever you want with it.
She gets in. He smiles as she tries the wheel.
Can we drive it? Will you teach me?

(a sigh) You know I do.
EXT. THE CARPENTERS ARMS - DAY
The Firm members headed in. Humming along with each other... "Maybe It's Because I'm A Londoner..." As Ian Barrie turns to scan the street behind him...
(a toast)
INT. THE CARPENTERS ARMS - DAY
Payne raises a glass. The Firm gathered for drinks. Payne at the bar, Ron one side, Reggie on the other.
There's an inherent threat in that one commanding syllable. It's the only word some need to hear.
What's that mean, the only word?
Final Shooting Draft
You're like Ford. Shell. Woolworths.
Woolworths. Interesting...
Kray is a brand. And an established reputation needs less maintenance. Less violence. People fall naturally in line. Clubs are asking for our protection before we even offer it.
(confirming) That's true. They are.
I suppose it leaves us with less straightening out to do.
That's right.
Ron going dark, considers the glass in his hand.
I happen to like a good straightener. Obliging someone on the cobbles. What's wrong with that?
I just think you could do better, Ron. It'll keep you all out of prison, I mean, none of us are getting any younger. (turns to Reggie) And you've got your lovely wife to think about.
Wife. Without warning, Ron smashes his glass into Payne's face. As Payne falls to the floor, Reggie grabs Ron who tries to get around him. Apoplectic at Payne:
I'm marking your card! This is a firm and I'm a face, understand? It's not fucking Woolworths!
Payne gets to his feet. Bleeding from a cut on his cheekbone.
Barrie, take him to get that sorted.
Final Shooting Draft
Barrie steps over, sets a hand on Payne's shoulder. As he guides him out with Reggie following.
I'll serve you up, you fucker!
Ron hurls a glass at Payne. It misses smashing the wall.
Ron! Enough!
He knows too much and I don't trust him. I want him dead.
Leslie's our partner and you want him dead? Are you taking your tablets?
Bugger the tablets! And bugger Payne the fucking Brain and his briefcase! Do you ever wonder what he keeps in there?!
No! What I wonder is, what you keep up here?
Reggie pokes Ron's forehead with his forefinger. Ron bats his hand away, resentful of the treatment.

(turns to the boys) Hey, Albert, do you know things about us? About Ron and me, about the Firm? Do you know how payments are made, how bonds are cashed, how jurors are made to look favorably upon us? (Donoghue just stares) Do you?!
Yes, I know.
Reggie looks toward Dickson. Final Shooting Draft
What about you, Jack? Do you know how we run frauds, how we pay off certain policeman on the last Thursday of every month?
Of course I do.
Reggie wheels on his brother.
Should we kill Jack, too? And Albert? All of them, and then each other?! Listen to yourself! You're stark staring mad!
Ron considers his brother rather coolly.
I take my Stematol. I know who I am. And I see you. And people who live in glass houses shouldn't throw stones.
It looks like Reggie will belt him. He heads out instead.
INT. SUITE - THE LONDON HILTON - NIGHT
Angelo Bruno pours two glasses of gin, neat. MOVE WITH him as he brings one glass to Reggie who sits by the big window overlooking London. They are alone. They both sip.
You know I never drank gin until I came here.
Next thing you'll be singing God Save the Queen. What did you want to see me about, Mr. Bruno?
Things are going well. We're very happy on my side of the pond.
Glad to hear it. We're happy also.
They clink glasses. Final Shooting Draft
Cheers... You realize I asked to see you alone?
Reggie nods, waits. Bruno gestures to a paper on the coffee table, the old The Picture We Dare Not Print edition.
In our line of work, it ain't good to be famous. Anonymous is the goal. (after a beat) It's Mr. Lansky, Reg. He sees a lot of potential in you, but he sees limitations in your brother Ron.
Maybe he needs to get to know him better. Come across for a chat.

A chat. You guys crack me up. (suddenly serious) You heard of the Gallo Brothers? From Brooklyn? (Reggie shakes his head) Similar loose cannon situation. Ron's your Joey. And it's not going to end well in either case.
What exactly are you asking me to do, Mr. Bruno?
I'm asking you to do something about
The words weigh heavy in the room. Reggie never takes his eyes off Bruno. Finally...
I can't do that. He's my brother.
Bruno just smiles, let's it go like it was nothing.
Final Shooting Draft INT. BEDROOM - CEDRA COURT - NIGHT
Frances wakes suddenly. Almost like she sensed something.
INT. MEDICINE CABINET - BATHROOM - CEDRA COURT - NIGHT
Frances swallowing a pill, looking at herself in the mirror.
INT. THE SOFA - CEDRA COURT - DAWN

Frances wrapped in a blanket, playing SOLITAIRE. Dawn's early light on the windows. She reacts to footsteps in the hall. The sound of fumbling at the lock and the door opens. Disheveled after a long night, Reggie enters.
Where've you been, Reggie?
Practising my trade. May I come in?
Frances stands, steps over.
I want a husband, not a visitor.
This is what being a club owner means: Odd hours at clubs.
As he pulls loose his tie, Frances shuts the door, considers the STEEL PLATE screwed to the inside.
You could call.
(turning on her) And say what?!
Don't shout at me!
Then don't meet me with questions!
Final Shooting Draft
(re: steel plate) What's this? It was here when I came home today.
It's for protection. From what?
What do you think? In case some bugger fires a gun through the door.
We can't live like gangsters!
I've news for you, Frankie! It's how club owners live as well! You can't have everything! Me on a leash! Ron kept away! Not everything!
She starts to cry. Angry, Reggie pulls off his jacket and shirt. He marches out the way he came in. She follows...
EXT. DOCKS - THE ISLE OF DOG - DAY
Thick and brutish Richardson gangster George Cornell looms over FULLER.
There's nothing I can do.
Fuller tries to step away. Cornell blocks him.
I ain't finished with you yet.
Mr. Kray takes care of this place.
Say Kray again I'll hurt you.
I have an agreement with Mr. Kray!
Final Shooting Draft
WHALLOP! Cornell breaks his nose with a single punch. Holds him up by his collar as Fuller's legs nearly go out.
I didn't ask if you had an agreement! I asked if you understood me!
As Fuller nods meekly, Cornell hits him again.
The Richardson gang were away, but George Cornell had crossed the river working a protection racket on their behalf. Ron's response would secure his place in gangster legend.
INT. CARPENTERS ARMS PUB - DAY
Fuller sits with Ron, nose heavily bandaged. Teddy and Leslie Holt here as well. Ron lifts the top of a CASK of TEA, smells it as Fuller waits nervously.
That's lovely. A bit of magic. Mum will be pleased.
My regards to her. And the other matter?
What other matter?
Ron looks to Teddy who shrugs. Realizing finally...
You mean this?
He taps Fuller on his broken nose. Fuller recoils.
I'm not giving out any refunds.
Teddy and Leslie have a good laugh at that one.
Can't you handle it yourself?
I pay you for protection.
What are you trying to say?
Final Shooting Draft Nothing.
No, you just said it. You come in here with your exotic tea and say that I'm not very good at my job.
Leslie Holt reacts. Fuller is in trouble.
I didn't mean that, Ron. You did.
Fuller gulps, in real trouble now. Ron relents.
Alright, I'll deal with this. You pay me two thousand pounds and I will have Geroge Cornell killed.
(alarmed) Who said anything about killing?!
You did. There's death in your eyes... Or are you threatening me? What?!
(to Teddy) Is he threatening me?
Teddy shrugs, not sure. Ron feels very dangerous now.
I'll pay. I'll pay.
(settling) There, good, and a deal's a deal.
EXT./INT. MERCEDES - WHITECHAPEL ROAD - NIGHT
Barrie at the wheel. Teddy Smith beside Ron in back. Ron looks out the window as they near the BLIND BEGGAR PUB. Final Shooting Draft
As the car stops, Ron checks on a PISTOL, adjusts his tie.
How do I look?
(scared) Fucking deadly. That's what Ron wanted to hear.
Back in a moment. Barrie, c'mon.
Before Barrie can ask what's up, Ron is out the door. Barrie looks to Smith who grins back at him like a loon. As Barrie realizes something is very wrong.
Is there anything you need to tell me?
INT. BLIND BEGGAR PUB - NIGHT

Ron barges in, marches along... A BARMAID by the taps. A FEW PEOPLE scattered about and at the end of the bar: Cornell sits with Albert Woods. Cornell sees Ron striding toward him -- Barrie enters beyond.

Well, look what the cat dragged in. Cornell barely has time to react to the gun Ron raises. BOOM! -- He shoots Cornell straight through the forehead. Blood sprays out the back. One eye closes and Cornell pitches face first onto the floor.
I'm not fat.
The BARMAID SCREAMS. Woods, the men at the table, in shock.
Ron, for fuck's sake...
Final Shooting Draft
Ron starts back the way he came in. Barrie draws his own gun, FIRES into the ceiling. That gets everyone diving, eyes down.
What are you looking at!? You didn't
see nothing! Ron exits with Barrie behind him. The Barmaid rises, looks around.
That was Ron Kray.
INT. PARLOR - 178 VALLANCE ROAD - NIGHT

Ron sitting in his favorite chair eating a piece of CAKE. Teddy and Barrie sitting to the left and right of him. Barrie bracing for the worst as the front door opens and Reggie and Donoghue enter. Reggie in a tux, obviously here from one of the clubs. Ron is happy to see him. Reggie!
Are you mad? (Ron frowns) The Blind Beggar? Cornell? It's on the fucking radio.
Violet Kray enters from the kitchen with tea for Ron, sees:
Hello, Mum.
Look how handsome you are!
The room frozen a beat. Violet is oblivious.
I'll get you a cup of tea. Thank you.
Final Shooting Draft As Violet exits, Reggie sits across from his brother.
(grins) You do look lovely, Reg.
Reggie ignores Teddy, looks to Barrie.
How many witnesses?
Reggie looks to Donoghue: Seven?!
Albert Woods was with Cornell. Plus five regulars, and that barmaid. The blonde? The one with the big fucking pair of eyes.
Oh, for fuck's sake...
(to Ron) And this is where you bring it? You bring it to Mum's?
(eating cake) Don't moan about it. It's done. What are we going to do? We?
Ron nods as he sips his tea.
Where's the weapon? Here.
Teddy holds out the gun he's been keeping between his legs. Reggie pushes it aside and out of sight as Violet returns with Reggie's tea.
Here you are. Would you like a piece of cake?
Final Shooting Draft
That would be lovely, Mum. Thank you.
As she heads back into the kitchen, Reggie looks to Ron.
I assume you were wearing those clothes you have on. Yeah.
(slurping his tea)
Where's his jacket?
Barrie gestures to his jacket folded on a chair.
I want you to burn that. And dump the pistol in the river. Right now. Fuck off.
Barrie gathers the jacket, takes the gun from Teddy. As he leaves, Reggie turns to Donoghue.

(to Barrie) Albert, whoever's was in that pub, you need to find out who they are and unravel anything they've said to the police. Get Big Pat and Bender and send them out there. That barmaid I know, right?
You know her.
Bring her to me. I'll deal with her myself.
Donoghue heads out. As Reggie settles on his brother, Violet returns with the cake.
Where's everybody gone?
Mum, it's late. I want them out of your hair. You should get some sleep. (to Ron) (MORE)
Come on, Ron, I'll give you a lift home. I am home.
I've made a bed up for him. (feels his head) He's not well. He's got a fever.
A beat as Ron considers Reggie: Your move.
Teddy. Would you like to stay the night with him?
A beat as Teddy considers Reggie, and looks to Violet.
Yes, thank you, that would be lovely, Violet.
It's all very cosy as Reggie considers.
Whatever he's done, he's your brother.
Reggie nods to himself, then reaches into his pocket for a bottle of pills. He sets it on the coffee table.
Here's your Stematol, mate. You should take that.
Ron regards the pills with disgust.
I have to go, Mum. I left the lads at work and we'll need to close up. So good night. Good night.
Reggie stands, then leans over Teddy.
(low) Teddy, be useful. When you take his trousers and shirt off tonight, will you burn them? And then check in with me.
Yeah...
Reggie pats him hard on the cheek, heads out.
Night, night. Good night.
As Ron watches him go, he points a 'finger gun', fires it.
He's lovely.
INT. TINTAGEL HOUSE - DAY
Moving in day, boxes being unpacked, an operation being set into motion. As Nipper walks past it all...
Even Scotland Yard couldn't ignore murder on the streets. Nipper was back on the case.
Nipper pins two photos to a large BLANK BOARD. One of Reggie and one of Ron. He turns to face TWENTY young DETECTIVES.
Gentlemen, your attention please. You've been told we are conducting a high level inquiry into a major corruption allegation. In fact, we are going down into the sewers after
Nipper lets that sit a moment.

Until further notice, do not park your cars on the street, always vary your routes home and the few of you who are married, consider sending your families away. Any man who has any objection, come and see me.
EXT. VALLANCE ROAD - DAY
Frances and Reggie walk along, Reg pointing things out. Final Shooting Draft

See that lamp post there? During the war that was the safety point. If you could run to that you knew you could make it into the air raid shelter before the bombs started dropping. Me and Ron stopped there to watch one night. Saw the old Stanton's house blown to bits with all them in it.
She looks up at the clouds.
It's a wonder you lived through it.
Ron especially. When trouble came he was always dragging behind.
He's got plenty of trouble now if the stories are true. I heard it on the street, Reggie. My own family and you didn't tell me.
I didn't want you to worry.
I don't worry about Ron. What do you plan to do about it?
I can't let him go to prison. Why not?
Why not? Because he's my brother.
He'll still be your brother in prison.
No. Frances, he belongs here. With his family. I've looked after Ron since we were boys.
He stops. She steps close to him, trying to get through. Final Shooting Draft
You can still look after him in prison. And he doesn't belong here. And you know it.
Right... You just don't understand -FRANCES Yes I do. What about you? Don't you want to be free? Free of him?
By letting him go to prison? Letting him lose his freedom? His life?

You don't understand!

Reggie can't handle it; he finds a way out.

She considers what he said. Then, fiercely...

She turns, starts to walk away from him...

Heaven, of course.

As he shuts the door on her.

Reggie sat alone at the bar. As the world weighs heavy on his shoulders, he realizes something.
EXT. 5TH FLOOR WINBOURNE TOWER BLOCK, WALTHAMSTOW - DAY

Who is it? As Ron considers him, considers the task at hand.
